agents.prompt: For each ambiguous element, generate candidate interpretations. Then apply a three-step verification: check grammatical agreement, semantic plausibility, and commonsense consistency. Keep only candidates passing all three. Next, for each remaining candidate, evaluate pragmatic plausibility: does the interpretation make sense given the question's context and typical real-world knowledge? Select the candidate with highest pragmatic plausibility. If tied, use most specific antecedent. Output the letter of the final candidate.

Construct a table with candidates as rows and clues (gender, number, syntactic role, semantic plausibility) as columns. For each cell, mark pass/fail. Then eliminate rows with any fail. If multiple rows remain, pick the row with the most passes. Show the table and reasoning in your trace. Output final answer as a single letter.

List every possible referent for the pronoun. For each referent, check gender agreement, number agreement, syntactic role compatibility, and semantic plausibility. Document each check with a pass/fail. Select the referent that passes all four. Then verify that no other referent also passes all four; if any does, explain which constraint it fails. Finally, output the option letter of your chosen referent.

Compare each pair of candidate antecedents. For each pair, apply constraints (gender, number, syntactic role, semantic plausibility) and select the better one. Continue pairwise until one winner remains. Output only the winner's letter.

Create a constraint checklist: gender, number, syntactic role, semantic plausibility. For each candidate, mark pass/fail on each constraint. Discard any candidate that fails a mandatory constraint (gender, number, syntactic role). From survivors, select the one with highest semantic plausibility; if tie, pick the one with most constraint passes overall. After selection, perform a numeric sanity check: count survivors and passes for the chosen candidate. If the chosen candidate has fewer passes than another survivor, or if any mandatory constraint was missed, reconsider and select the survivor with the most passes. Present reasoning as structured list per candidate. Final answer: single option letter.
